subject: Mathematics
question_text: If an isosceles triangle has more than one line of symmetry, then it need not be an equilateral triangle.
answer_explanation: The given word is ‘MATHEMATICS’. The letter M has one line of symmetry.    The letter A has one line of symmetry.    The letter T has one line of symmetry.    The letter H has two line of symmetry.    The letter E has one line of symmetry.    The letter I has two line of symmetry.    The letter C has one line of symmetry.    And the letter S has no line of symmetry.    Hence, only letter ‘S’ in word ‘MATHEMATICS’ has no line of symmetry. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 65. | Page 142 ####  Video TutorialsVIEW ALL ") ####

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If an isosceles triangle has more than one line of symmetry, then it must be an equilateral triangle.
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw a line segment AB = 7 cm.   2. With A as centre and radius more than half of AB, draw arcs, one on each side of AB.   3. With B as centre and same radius as before, draw arcs, cutting the previously drawn arcs at X and Y respectively.   4. Now join XY, meeting AB at O. Thus, XY is the perpendicular bisector of AB. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 75. | Page 144    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×

subject: Mathematics
question_text: A square and a rectangle have the same number of lines of symmetry.
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw a line segment AB = 6.5 cm.   2. With A and B as centres and radius more than 12 (AB), draw arcs one on each side of AB, these arcs cuts each other at E and F.   3. Join EF, which cuts AB at Q.   4. With centres A and Q and radius more than 12 AQ, draw arcs one on each side of AB, these arcs cuts each other at G and H.   5. Join GH, which cuts AB at P.   6. Similarly, with centres B and Q and radius more than 12 (BQ), draw arcs one on each side of AB, these arcs cuts each other at I and J.   7. Join IJ, which cuts AB at R.   8. Now, point P, Q and R divide the line segment AB in four equal parts. Thus AP = PQ = QR = RB. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 76. | Page 144    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×
provenance_excerpt: A square and a rectangle have the same number of lines of symmetry.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: A circle has only 16 lines of symmetry.
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw ∠BAC = 140° with the help of protractor.   2. With A as centre and any convenient radius, draw an arc, cutting AC and AB at Q and P respectively.   3. With centre P and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑃⁢𝑄), draw an arc.   4. With centre Q and the same radius as before, draw another arc, cutting the previously drawn arc at a point S.   5. Join AS and produce it to any point X. Then, ray AX bisects ∠CAB. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 77. | Page 144    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×

subject: Mathematics
question_text: A 45o - 45o - 90o set-square and a protractor have the same number of lines of symmetry. AEAC
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw ∠AOB = 65° with the help of protractor.   2. Draw a ray O’X.   3. With O as centre and any radius, draw an arc cutting OA and OB at C and D respectively.   4. With O’ as centre and with the same radius, draw an arc, cutting O’X at P.   5. With P as centre and radius equal to CD, cut the arc through P at Q.   6. Join O’Q and produce it to Y. Then ∠YO’X is the required angle equal to ∠AOB. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 78. | Page 144    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×

subject: Mathematics
question_text: It is possible to draw two bisectors of a given angle.
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw ∠AOB = 80° with the help of protractor.   2. With centre O and any radius, draw an arc, meeting ⟶𝑂⁢𝐵 and ⟶𝑂⁢𝐵 at P and Q respectively.   3. With centres P and Q and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑃⁢𝑄), draw arcs which cuts each other at R.   4. Join OR which bisects PQ at S.   5. With centres P and S and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑃⁢𝑆), draw arcs which cuts each other at U.   6. Join OU.   7. With centres Q and S and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑄⁢𝑆), draw arcs which cuts each other at V.   8. Join OV. Thus, ∠AOV, ∠VOR, ∠ROU and ∠UOB divide ∠BOA in four equal parts and ∠AOV = ∠VOR = ∠ROU = 20° shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 79. | Page 144    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×

subject: Mathematics
question_text: A regular octagon has 10 lines of symmetry.
answer_explanation: We draw perpendicular to l through P, using **(i)** Set square **Steps of construction are as follows:** **Step I:** A line l and a point P are given. Note that P is on the line l.    **Step II:** Place a ruler with one of its edges along l. Hold it firmly.    **Step III:** Place a set square with one of its edges along the already aligned edge of the ruler, such that the right angled corner is in contant with the ruler.    **Step IV:** Slide the set square along the edge of ruler unit its right angled corner concides with P.    **Step V:** Hold the set square along the edge of ruler unit its right angled corner coincides with P.  **(ii)** Protractor **Step I:** A line l and a point P are given. Note that P is on the line l.    **Step II:** Place the protractor on the line, such that its base line coincides with l and its centre falls on P. **Step III:** Mark a point B against the 90° mark on the protractor. **Step IV:** Remove the protractor and draw a line m passing through P and B. Then, PB ⊥ l.    **(iii)** Ruler and Compass **Step I:** Given, a point P on a line l.    **Step II:** With P as centre and a convenient radius, construct an arc intersecting the line l at two points A and B.    **Step III:** With A and B as centres and a radius greater than AP construct two arcs, which cut each other at Q.    **Step IV:** Join PQ. Then, PQ is perpendicular to l.    Hence, we are able to draw one perpendicular line. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 80. | Page 144    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Sel
provenance_excerpt: A regular octagon has 10 lines of symmetry.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Infinitely many perpendiculars can be drawn to a given ray.
answer_explanation: We draw perpendicular to m from P, using **(i)** Set squares **Step I:** Let m be the given line and p be a point outside m. Now, extended line m on both the sides.    **Step II:** Place a set square on m, such that one arm of its right angle aligns along m.    **Step III:** Place a ruler along the edge opposite to the tight angle of the set square.    **Step IV:** Hold the ruler fixed. Slide the set square along the ruler till the point P touches the other arm of the set square.    **Step V:** Join PM along the edge through P, meeting m at O. Now, PO ⊥ m.  **(ii)** Protractor **Step I:** Let m be the given line and P be a point putside m.    **Step II:** Place the protractor on point P, such that its centre coincides with point P. **Step III:** Mark a point B againt the 90° mark on the protractor. **Step IV:** Remove the protractor and draw a line l passing through P and B which intersects line m at O. Then, PO ⊥ m.  **(iii)** Ruler and compass **Step I:** Given, a line m and a point P, not on it. Extend the given line in both directions.    **Step II:** With P as entre, draw an arc which intersects line m at two points A and B.    **Step III:** With A and B as centres and the same radius draw two arcs which intersect at a point, say Q, on the other side.    **Step IV:** Join PQ.    Thus, PQ is perpendicular to m. We are able to draw one perpendicular line. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 81. | Page 145    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in
provenance_excerpt: Infinitely many perpendiculars can be drawn to a given ray.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Infinitely many perpendicular bisectors can be drawn to a given ray.
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw a circle with centre O of radius 6 cm.   2. Now, draw a diameter AB of the circle.   3. With the centre B and the radius more than 12⁢(̂𝐴⁢𝐵), draw the arcs on each side of AB.   4. With the centre A and the same radius, draw the arcs on each side of AB which cuts the previously drawn arc at X and Y.   5. Now join OX and OY and produce them to any points P and Q.   Thus PQ bisects the diameter of a circle. Yes, the perpendicular PQ contains another diameter of this circle. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 82. | Page 145    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Is there any line of symmetry in the Fig. 9.12? If yes, draw all the lines of symmetry. A B                  D C
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. With centre Y and any radius draw an arc which cuts ZY at P and XY at Q.   2. With centre Q and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑃⁢𝑄) draw an arc.   3. With centre P and same radius draw an arc which cuts the previously drawn arc at R.   4. Join YR and produce it to any point S. Thus ⟶𝑌⁢𝑆 is the bisector of ∠XYZ. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 83. | Page 145    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×

subject: Mathematics
question_text: In Fig. 9.13, PQRS is a rectangle. State the lines of symmetry of the rectangle. P               A             Q B D S              C              R E AD ACCA EE    
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw a ray ⟶𝑂⁢𝐴   2. With O as centre and any convenient radius draw an arc meeting the rav OA at X.   3. With X as centre and the same radius draw an arc which cuts the previously drawn arc at U.   4. Join OU and produce it to any point B. So ∠BOA = 60°.   5. Now with centre X and U and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑈⁢𝑋) draw the arcs on the same side of ∠BOA which cuts each other at V.   6. Join OV and produce it any point C as well as it cuts the arc ̂𝑈⁢𝑋 at W.   7. Again with the centre X and W and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑊⁢𝑋) draw the arcs on the same side of ∠BOA which cuts each other at Z.   8. Join OZ and produce it to any point D.   9. With the centre U and W and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑈⁢𝑊) draw the arcs on the same side of ∠BOA with cuts each other at the point P.   10. Join OP and produce it to any point E. Now with the help of protractor we observe that each angle i.e, ∠BOE = ∠EOC = ∠COD = ∠DOA = 15°. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 84. | Page 145    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Write all the capital letters of the English alphabets which have more than one lines of symmetry.
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw a line AB of any length.   2. Draw an arc of 180° which meets the ray ⟶𝑂⁢𝐴 at P and ray ⟶𝑂⁢𝐵 at Q.   3. Taking P and Q as centres and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑃⁢𝑄), draw two arcs which intersect each other at R.   4. Join OR and produce it to any point C.   5. Thus, ⟶𝑂⁢𝐶 bisect the straight angle. Now, on measuring each angle we get, ∠BOC = ∠AOC = 90°. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 85. | Page 145    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×
provenance_excerpt: Write all the capital letters of the English alphabets which have more than one lines of symmetry.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Write the letters of the word ‘MATHEMATICS’ which have no line of symmetry.
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw ∠AOB = 90° with the help of protractor.   2. Draw an arc with centre O and any radius, which cuts the ray OB and OA at U and Y.   3. With centres U and Y draw the arcs with radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑈⁢𝑌), which cuts each other at Q.   4. Join OQ and produce it to any point D as well as it cuts the arc ̂𝑌⁢𝑈 at W.   5. With centres U and W and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑊⁢𝑈) draw the arcs which cuts each other at P.   6. Join OP and produce it to any point E.   7. Now with centres Y and W and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑌⁢𝑊) draw the arcs which cuts each other at R.   8. Join OR and produce it to any point C. On measuring, we have ∠BOD = ∠AOD = 45° and ∠BOE = ∠EOD = ∠DOC = ∠COA = 22⁢12∘ shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 86. | Page 145    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×
provenance_excerpt: Write the letters of the word ‘MATHEMATICS’ which have no line of symmetry.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Write the number of lines of symmetry in each letter of the word ‘SYMMETRY’.
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw a ray ⟶𝐵⁢𝐶   2. With B as centre and any radius draw an arc which cuts BC at P.   3. With centre P and same radius, draw an arc which cuts the previously drawn arc at Q.   4. Similarly, with Q as centre and same radius draw an another arc which cuts the previously drawn arc at R.   5. With centre R and Q and same radius draw the arcs which cuts each other at S.   6. Join BS which cuts the arc RP at T.   7. With centre P and T and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑃⁢𝑇) draw the arcs which cuts each other at U.   8. Join BU and produce it to a point A. Thus ∠ABC = 45°.   9. Now, with B as centre draw another arc which cuts the line AB at X.   10. With centre X and same radius draw an arc which cut the arc drawn in step (ix) at Y.   11. With centre X and Y and radius more than 12⁢(̂𝑋⁢𝑌) draw two arcs which cuts each other at Z.   12. Join BZ and produced it to a point D. Thus ∠ABD = 30°. On measuring, we get ∠DBC = 75° shaalaa.com  Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 87. | Page 145    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Match the following: Shape              Number of lines of symmetry (i)      Isosceles triangle                      (a) 6 (ii)     Square                                  (b) 5 (iii)     Kite                                    (c) 4 (iv)      Equilateral triangle                    (d) 3 (v)      Rectangle                               (e) 2 (vi)      Regular hexagon                         (f)    1 (vii)      Scalene triangle                        (g) 0
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw a line segment AB = 6 cm   2. With centre A and radius more than 12⁢(𝐴⁢𝐵) draw the arcs on each side of AB.   3. With centre B and same radius draw the arcs on each side of AB, which cuts the previously drawn arcs at X and Y.   4. Join XY which cuts the line segment AB at O.   Thus XY is the perpendicular bisector of AB.   On measuring the two parts, we get AO = OB = 3 cm. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 88. | Page 145    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Open your geometry box. There are some drawing tools. Observe them and complete the following table: Name of the tool                           Number of lines of symmetry (i)      The Ruler                                          _______ (ii)     The Divider                                        _______ (iii)     The Compasses                                      _______ (iv)      The Protactor                                      _______ (v)      Triangular piece with two equal sides              _______ (vi)      Triangular piece with unequal sides                _______    EEA BE AEAC
answer_explanation: **Steps of construction:**   1. Draw a line segment AB = 10 cm.   2. With A and B as centres and radius more than 12⁢(𝐴⁢𝐵), draw arcs on each side AB, these arcs cuts each other at R at S.   3. Join RS, which cuts AB at a point O.   4. Now, with centres A and O and radius more than 12⁢(𝐴⁢𝑂), draw arcs on each side of AB, these arcs cuts each other at P and Q.   5. Join PQ, which cuts AB at M.   6. Similarly, with centres B and O and radius more than 12⁢(𝐵⁢𝑂), draw arcs on each side of AB, these arcs cuts each other at T and U.   7. Join TU, which cuts AB at N. Thus the measurement of each i.e., AM, MO, ON and NB is equal to 2.5 cm. shaalaa.com     Chapter 9: Symmetry and Practical Geometry - Exercise  Chapter 9 Symmetry and Practical Geometry   Exercise | Q 89. | Page 145    Question Bank with Solutions   * ")    * ")   * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *     *    *    *    *    *  Question Paper Solutions   *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  Other Resources   *    *    *    *    * ")   *    *    *    *    *    *  © 2026 Shaalaa.com  |  |  Share   0 0 0 0 0 Notifications      #### Select a course CANCEL   Login  email:   password:   Log in  Course    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *    *  ×
